page:
# Welcome to Introduction to Marine Biology
This course is organized as a two-week sequence of daily lessons, applied activities, and milestone assessments. Each module follows the outline of the intensive schedule, so the fastest way to stay on track is to move through the modules in order.
## How to Use This Course
1. Review the syllabus and assessment overview first. 2. Open each daily lesson page before class or study time. 3. Record key observations, diagrams, and data in your lab notebook every day. 4. Complete the midweek quiz after Day 5. 5. Use the final module to prepare your presentation and reflection essay.
## What You Will Study
- Ocean structure, geography, and chemistry - Planktonic life and energy transfer - Coral reefs, estuaries, mangroves, and deep-sea systems - Marine mammals, sharks, rays, sea turtles, and conservation policy
## Success Strategies
- Build a running vocabulary list for new scientific terms. - Sketch processes such as nutrient cycling, food-web transfer, and migration routes. - Compare ecosystems rather than studying each one in isolation. - Tie every topic back to a conservation or management question.
## Before Day 1
Bring a notebook or digital equivalent, review the course schedule, and be ready to discuss one reason the ocean matters to human societies.
